reasoning: Fabiano Caruana's recent Elo of 2789 requires a gain of around 27.9 points (~1% of 2789) to cross the threshold, meaning he would have to reach an Elo of approximately 2817. For established top players with a rating K-factor of 10, gaining 28 points requires scoring almost 3 full points above expectation, which is mathematically akin to executing a 3000+ performance rating over a string of top-level long-format games. This is an extremely rare, historic outcome—comparable to his famous 2014 Sinquefield Cup run. Over the very short timeline toward Nov 2025, reaching this mark is nearly impossible as it is bounded by the monthly publication cycle of FIDE ratings. Furthermore, the Nov 2 and Nov 25 resolution dates will almost certainly reflect the exact same November 1 FIDE list, giving no extra time to accrue rating. Even projecting to the January 2026 list, the massive and consistent winning streak required keeps the realistic likelihood extremely low. Uncertainty strictly increases for the final date to account for more events taking place.
batch_queries: According to Wikipedia, will Fabiano Caruana have an Elo rating on each resolution date listed below that's at least 1% higher than on 2025-10-26?
